metric_value: Literally hundreds of CIS suppliers in the market in 1996; study profiles over 40
Second Aberdeen CIS report; first published May 1995; 1996 edition priced at US$695
Three audiences: (1) business executives choosing CIS, (2) IS executives implementing CIS, (3) CIS suppliers analyzing market position

metric_name: CIS module: Customer Support / Internal Helpdesk
metric_value: Issue tracking, escalation, knowledge base for internal IT and external customer support
Dispatch, scheduling, parts management, on-site service tracking
Contact management, opportunity tracking, forecasting, sales territory management
Outbound/inbound calling campaigns, lead management, scripting

metric_name: CTI as key CIS enabling technology
metric_value: Computer-Telephony Integration identified as important related technology for CIS; links customer phone interactions with data systems
Aberdeen explains 'unexpected implications' of Internet-enabled CIS; web-based customer interaction beginning to emerge
notes: 1996 was early; implications of web-based CRM still being discovered.

metric_value: Profiled CIS supplier; founded 1993 by Tom Siebel; focused on enterprise sales force automation; early-stage high-growth vendor
notes: Siebel went on to dominate the CRM market before Oracle acquisition.

metric_value: Profiled CIS supplier; strong in IT helpdesk/service management; Action Request System product
notes: Remedy acquired by BMC Software in 2002.

metric_value: Profiled CIS supplier; focused on customer support and CRM; later acquired by Nortel for $2.1B in 1998

metric_name: Aberdeen warning on CIS market consolidation
metric_value: Companies rushing to install CIS systems cannot afford to make mistakes. Market filled with hundreds of suppliers — only a few will be around over the long term
Growth and profit-oriented companies gaining market share through coordinated sales tactics and superior customer service via CIS

metric_name: Prediction: CIS market will consolidate dramatically
metric_value: Most of hundreds of CIS suppliers will not survive long-term; only a few will remain to support users; buyers must choose right the first time

metric_name: Prediction: Internet will transform CIS delivery
metric_value: Internet-enabled CIS will create unexpected implications; future CIS systems will leverage web technologies

metric_name: Outcome: CRM market consolidation around Siebel/Oracle/SAP
metric_value: Siebel Systems became dominant CRM vendor with ~$1.5B revenue before Oracle acquisition for $5.8B (2006); SAP CRM and Salesforce.com (2000) also emerged; hundreds of small vendors were consolidated or disappeared
notes: Aberdeen's consolidation prediction proved entirely correct. Clarify ($2.1B to Nortel 1998), Scopus (to Siebel 1998), Vantive (to PeopleSoft 1999) all confirmed pattern.

metric_value: Salesforce.com (founded 1999) pioneered SaaS/web-based CRM and reached $300M revenue by 2006; web-based CRM became standard; prediction proved highly accurate
notes: Aberdeen's 1996 prediction about Internet implications for CIS was correct but underestimated the disruption; SaaS CRM completely transformed the market by 2010.

metric_name: CIS supplier profiles count in 1996 study
metric_value: Over 40 CIS software suppliers profiled; 5 systems integrators profiled; 3 hardware/platform suppliers profiled
notes: Including Applix, Astea, Aurum, Clarify, Remedy, Siebel, Vantive, Scopus among others.
